response:
SECTION 1  
there are two tree hollows in a cedar. a squirrel sitting in front of the second hollow said:  
1) there are no nuts in the other hollow,  
2) there is at least one nut in one of the hollows.  

SECTION 2  
red squirrels always tell the truth, while gray squirrels always lie.  
what color is this squirrel?  

SECTION 3  
the first statement says there are no nuts in the other hollow,  
the second statement says there is at least one nut in one of the hollows.  

SECTION 4  
if the squirrel were red, both statements would be true,  
but if the squirrel were gray, both statements would be false.  

SECTION 5  
the first statement is false if there are nuts in the other hollow,  
the second statement is false if there are no nuts in any of the hollows.  

SECTION 6  
the squirrel must be gray, because both statements cannot be true.  
the squirrel is gray.
